Feln PoIlrlcnr- PRACTICEs CourraISSIoN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 2,2007
Marissa Stutz
Ethics Office
Los Angeles Unified School District
333 S. Beaudry Avenue, 20m Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90017
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-06-230
Dear Ms. Stutz:
Re:
We are in receipt of your request for written advice regarding the gift disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Commissio" ."gututio n 1g329 (copy
enclosed) establishes certain criteria that must be met before the ageicy will frovide
formal written advice or informal written assistance.
Your request does not provide sufficient material facts to qualifu for formal
written advice. (See regulation I 8329(bX2)(B).) We recogn ize tixas an ethics officer
of the Los Angelds Unified School District ("LAUSD') v"u have a duty to aJrise others
andmay request informal assistance under regulation t-tizo1c11t1. Hoivever, based on
the information provided in your faxed requests dated oecemberi 0,2006, id Jun ury
79,2007, it appears you may be requesting advice related to the duties of other, unnamed
officials and/or that your question may be hypothetical in nature. (Regulation
18329(c)@)(C) and (D).) Further, the information you have provided is insufficient and
too vague to permit us to provide informal written assistance. (Regulation
18329(c)@)(F). Therefore, we decline to provide written assistance at this time and are
withdrawing your request.
If you wish to resubmit your request, please provide the following information:
1. 
The name(s) and title(s) of the official(s) who provided the meals.
2. 
The name(s) and title(s) of the official(s) who received the meals.
3' 
Whether you are authoized to request advice on behalf of the official(s).
' Go"ernment Code sections 8 1 000 * 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2,sections
18109 - 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


I
Our File No. W-06-230
page 2
4' 
The specific circumstances sulrounding the event(s) at which the meals
were provided. For example, did the official(s) receive the meals in the
course of their official duties and would LAUSD have reimbursed them
for the cost of the meals absent the payment by the official(s) who used an
agency expense account to pay for the meals?
For your information, we have enclosed a Commission fact sheet thatprovides a
general discussion of the Act's provisions on gifts. If you have questions coneerning this
letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
/t'

By: CarlaWardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.conclusion: Your request does not provide sufficient material facts to qualify for formal written advice. We decline to provide written assistance at this time and are withdrawing your request.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether certain meals provided to LAUSD officials constitute gifts subject to disclosure under the Political Reform Act's gift disclosure provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide formal or informal written advice because the request lacked sufficient material facts and contained vague or hypothetical questions about unnamed officials' duties. The FPPC invited resubmission with specific information about the officials involved and circumstances of the meals.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination/withdrawal letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; extracted synthetic versions from body text. Letter dated February 2, 2007 but references File No. W-06-230 from 2006. Document contains OCR artifacts but content is substantially readable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether certain meals provided to LAUSD officials constitute gifts subject to disclosure under the Political Reform Act's gift disclosure provisions.

CONCLUSION: Your request does not provide sufficient material facts to qualify for formal written advice. We decline to provide written assistance at this time and are withdrawing your request.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined an ethics officer's request for advice regarding gift disclosure provisions under the Political Reform Act due to insufficient and vague information, and requested additional specific details about the officials and circumstances involved if the requester wishes to resubmit.
